reasoning:
Looking at this problem through the lens of "whizgig" - a spinning toy that maintains momentum through intermittent pulls rather than constant force.

The core insight: Traditional retirement systems fail irregular earners because they treat income consistency as a prerequisite rather than designing for its absence. A whizgig doesn't need constant energy input - it stores kinetic momentum and keeps spinning between pulls.

The fundamental problem is that we've coupled retirement security to *money*, which is inherently volatile. But everyone has one resource distributed with perfect equality: **time**.

```json
{
  "label": "Rotation Compact: Time-Banked Care",
  "design_principles": "Decouple retirement from money entirely. Trade volatility in earnings for stability in time. Care given = care received. Income irrelevant.",
  "core_mechanism": "Working adults contribute weekly service hours to current retirees; hours are ledger-banked with appreciation and redeemed as care services upon their own retirement.",
  "how_it_works": "Mandatory enrollment in regional Care Compact at first income. Contribute 4 hrs/week of verified elder services (meals, errands, companionship, tech help). Hours logged via app with recipient confirmation. Credits accrue 1:1 plus 2% annual appreciation. Can bank 52 weeks ahead or make up 104 weeks in arrears—allowing shift during income-volatile periods. At 65+, receive equivalent services from younger cohort. Unused hours bequeathable. Minimum services guaranteed via 5% overhead pool from all contributors.",
  "what_is_new": "Removes money entirely from basic retirement security. Income volatility becomes irrelevant—a gig worker earning $500 one month and $5000 the next contributes identical hours. No savings, no investment risk, no contribution minimums.",
  "why_it_works": "Income is volatile; time is not. Everyone has exactly 168 hours weekly regardless of earnings. Care needs are stable and predictable. By trading labor-now for labor-later rather than money-now for money-later, we eliminate every source of volatility: market risk, inflation, insufficient savings, inconsistent contributions. Creates intergenerational solidarity as side effect.",
  "why_it_fails": "Doesn't solve housing, medical, or other money-requiring retirement expenses—only care services. Demographic risk if young cohort shrinks. Time equality is fiction: 4 hours costs a stressed single parent more than a flexible freelancer. Quality enforcement is hard. Effectively mandatory labor, which some will find coercive.",
  "medium_term": "Care Compacts emerge as supplement to financial retirement. Elder loneliness and care poverty decline sharply. Young adults develop relationships with elders. Gig workers gain first real retirement pathway.",
  "long_term_vision": "Care poverty eliminated. Aging becomes a phase of receiving abundant human attention rather than depending on savings adequacy. Financial retirement becomes supplementary for discretionary spending, not essential for survival. Intergenerational isolation ends as millions form cross-age relationships through mandatory service. The anxiety of 'will I have enough' disappears for basic care needs."
}
```

solution.label: Rotation Compact: Time-Banked Care
solution.design_principles: Decouple retirement from money entirely. Trade volatility in earnings for stability in time. Care given = care received. Income irrelevant.
solution.core_mechanism: Working adults contribute weekly service hours to current retirees; hours are ledger-banked with appreciation and redeemed as care services upon their own retirement.
solution.how_it_works: Mandatory enrollment in regional Care Compact at first income. Contribute 4 hrs/week of verified elder services (meals, errands, companionship, tech help). Hours logged via app with recipient confirmation. Credits accrue 1:1 plus 2% annual appreciation. Can bank 52 weeks ahead or make up 104 weeks in arrears—allowing shift during income-volatile periods. At 65+, receive equivalent services from younger cohort. Unused hours bequeathable. Minimum services guaranteed via 5% overhead pool from all contributors.
solution.what_is_new: Removes money entirely from basic retirement security. Income volatility becomes irrelevant—a gig worker earning $500 one month and $5000 the next contributes identical hours. No savings, no investment risk, no contribution minimums.
solution.why_it_works: Income is volatile; time is not. Everyone has exactly 168 hours weekly regardless of earnings. Care needs are stable and predictable. By trading labor-now for labor-later rather than money-now for money-later, we eliminate every source of volatility: market risk, inflation, insufficient savings, inconsistent contributions. Creates intergenerational solidarity as side effect.
solution.why_it_fails: Doesn't solve housing, medical, or other money-requiring retirement expenses—only care services. Demographic risk if young cohort shrinks. Time equality is fiction: 4 hours costs a stressed single parent more than a flexible freelancer. Quality enforcement is hard. Effectively mandatory labor, which some will find coercive.
solution.medium_term: Care Compacts emerge as supplement to financial retirement. Elder loneliness and care poverty decline sharply. Young adults develop relationships with elders. Gig workers gain first real retirement pathway.
solution.long_term_vision: Care poverty eliminated. Aging becomes a phase of receiving abundant human attention rather than depending on savings adequacy. Financial retirement becomes supplementary for discretionary spending, not essential for survival. Intergenerational isolation ends as millions form cross-age relationships through mandatory service. The anxiety of 'will I have enough' disappears for basic care needs.
